title: 10 medicinal constituents of Dysosma pleiantha (chinese mayapple)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Dysosma pleiantha, also known as Chinese mayapple, betulinic acid is a natural compound extracted from its root. This compound has been found to exhibit significant anti-inflammatory and antitumor activities, making it a promising agent for the treatment of various diseases. Additionally, studies have shown that betulinic acid can inhibit the growth of cancer cells while having minimal toxicity towards normal cells, suggesting potential therapeutic applications in oncology.

Dysosma pleiantha, also known as Chinese Mayapple, ursolic acid is a bioactive compound isolated from its rhizome and leaves. It has been extensively studied for its potential pharmacological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anticancer properties. Ursolic acid has also been reported to possess cardio-protective effects and can inhibit the growth of certain cancer cells by inducing apoptosis.

Dysosma pleiantha, also known as Chinese Mayapple, oleanolic acid is a triterpenoid saponin compound extracted from the plant's leaves and stems. It has been shown to exhibit significant antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential agent for treating various diseases such as cancer, cardiovascular disorders, and respiratory infections.

Dysosma pleiantha, also known as Chinese Mayapple, quercetin is a type of flavonoid compound found in its root. Quercetin has been traditionally used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. It has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a potential natural remedy for the prevention and treatment of chronic diseases.

Dysosma pleiantha, also known as Chinese Mayapple, kaempferol is a flavonoid compound isolated from its roots and leaves. Kaempferol has been found to exhibit various biological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. It has also been shown to possess anti-cancer effects by inhibiting the proliferation of certain cancer cells and inducing apoptosis. Further research on kaempferol may lead to its development as a potential therapeutic agent for treating diseases.

Dysosma pleiantha, also known as Chinese Mayapple, gallic acid is a naturally occurring compound found in its leaves and stems. This acidic phenolic compound has been widely studied for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Gallic acid has also been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities, making it a promising candidate for the development of new therapeutic agents.

Dysosma pleiantha, also known as Chinese Mayapple, vanillin is a unique and distinctive fragrant compound derived from its rhizomes. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat various ailments. The extract of Dysosma pleiantha has been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. Its sweet and pleasant aroma is similar to that of vanilla, hence the name vanillin, making it a popular ingredient in perfumes and fragrances.

Dysosma pleiantha, also known as Chinese Mayapple, ferulic acid is a type of polyphenolic compound that has been isolated from its rhizomes. This bioactive compound has been reported to exhibit numerous pharmacological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Ferulic acid has also been found to possess cardioprotective effects by reducing lipid peroxidation and improving blood flow in the heart. Its potential therapeutic applications include treating cardiovascular disease, cancer, and other oxidative stress-related disorders.

Dysosma pleiantha, also known as Chinese mayapple, p-coumaric acid is a bioactive compound isolated from its roots. Studies have shown that this phenolic acid exhibits significant antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, making it a potential natural remedy for various diseases. Its ability to scavenge free radicals and inhibit the growth of pathogens has led researchers to explore its therapeutic applications in medicine.

Dysosma pleiantha, also known as Chinese Mayapple, caffeic acid is a bioactive compound isolated from its roots. It has been found to exhibit various pharmacological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Studies have also shown that it can inhibit the growth of cancer cells and induce apoptosis in certain types of tumors, making it a potential candidate for cancer treatment. Additionally, caffeic acid has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its therapeutic effects on respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.

intro: Dysosma pleiantha, also known as Chinese Mayapple, has active constituents such as iridoids, alkaloids, and flavonoids. These constituents impart anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties to the plant, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. The iridoids, in particular, have been shown to exhibit anti-diabetic and hypolipidemic effects, potentially helping individuals manage type 2 diabetes and high cholesterol levels. Additionally, the alkaloids have been found to possess analgesic and sedative properties, providing relief from pain and promoting relaxation.
intro_desc: Dysosma pleiantha, also known as Chinese Mayapple, has active constituents such as alkaloids, terpenoids, and saponins. These compounds are responsible for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The constituents can improve people's lives by providing relief from pain and inflammation, supporting the immune system, and protecting against oxidative stress and infections. For instance, its anti-inflammatory effects can alleviate arthritis symptoms, while its antibacterial properties can aid in wound healing.
